mission.title: Install and Configure Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE)
mission.description: Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Vultr's infrastructure. It provides automatic scaling, load balancing, and integration with Vultr's cloud services. Key trade-offs include reliance on Vultr's ecosystem and potential vendor lock-in.

steps.title: Install the Vultr CLI
steps.description:
The Vultr CLI is required to interact with the Vultr API for creating and managing Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/vultr/vultr-cli/releases/download/v1.0.0/vultr-cli_linux_amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf vultr-cli_linux_amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv vultr-cli /usr/local/bin/

vultr-cli version

```

> Download the latest Vultr CLI
> Extract the downloaded tarball
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH
> Verify the installation

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Vultr with the specified version and node count.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s create --region 'ewr' --version '1.30' --node-count 3 --plan 'vc2-4c-8gb'

```

> Replace 'ewr' with your preferred region

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s kubeconfig --cluster-id <your-cluster-id> > ~/.kube/config

```

> Replace <your-cluster-id> with the actual cluster ID

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are ready and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' state

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up monitoring and autoscaling for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloud-provider/vultr/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/monitoring/master/monitoring.yaml

```

> Deploy the cluster autoscaler
> Deploy monitoring tools

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This will remove the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s delete --cluster-id <your-cluster-id>

```

> Replace <your-cluster-id> with the actual cluster ID

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
Ensure that any orphaned resources such as load balancers or persistent volumes are also deleted.

```bash

vultr-cli loadbalancer delete --all

```

> Delete all load balancers

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup your data, upgrade the cluster, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces > backup.yaml

vultr-cli k8s upgrade --cluster-id <your-cluster-id> --version '1.30'

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup all resources
> Upgrade to the specified version
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' state after the upgrade

troubleshooting.title: Error: Unable to connect to the cluster
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig file may not be set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Re-run the kubeconfig setup command and ensure the cluster ID is correct.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Node not ready
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The node may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Check the node's resource allocation and consider resizing the node.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Load balancer not created
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service type may not be set to LoadBalancer.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service manifest specifies type: LoadBalancer.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Cluster autoscaler not functioning
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster autoscaler may not be configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the autoscaler logs for errors and ensure the correct permissions are set.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Vultr regions and improved load balancer integration.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy API keys.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your API keys to the new format and ensure your configurations are compatible.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with all nodes in 'Ready' state when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
